---
title: "Container Policy"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
---

::: {.callout-note}
🇫🇷 [Version française](../container-policy.qmd)
:::

## Mandatory rules

- Every Nextflow process declares its container with an **exact tag**
- Never `latest`
- `.def` files are versioned in the repo
- `.sif` files are **not** committed (archived on HPC or regenerated)
- Every container has a `--version` test in a bats file
- Every containerised process has an nf-test test runnable with `-profile apptainer`

---

## Authorised image sources

| Source | Usage |
|--------|-------|
| **BioContainers** | Standard bioinformatics images, tagged by version |
| **Seqera Containers / Wave** | Built on the fly from conda/bioconda |
| **Team registry** | Custom images — versioned `.def` required |

---

## The two test levels

### Level 1 — container alone

```bash
# Verify the tool exists and has the right version
apptainer exec fastqc_0.12.1.sif fastqc --version

# Verify the tool works without $HOME mounted
apptainer exec --no-home fastqc_0.12.1.sif fastqc --version
```

Implemented with bats (`container_<tool>.bats`).

### Level 2 — Nextflow process in its container

```groovy
process FASTQC {
    container 'biocontainers/fastqc:0.12.1--hdfd78af_0'
    ...
}
```

```bash
nf-test test fastqc.nf.test --profile apptainer
```

---

## Apptainer vs Docker differences in HPC

| Topic | Docker | Apptainer |
|-------|--------|-----------|
| Bind mounts | configurable | `/home`, `/tmp` mounted by default |
| Permissions | root inside the container | user's UID |
| Writing to `/` | possible | read-only by default |
| `$HOME` available | depends on config | yes, unless `--no-home` |

Always test with Apptainer in an HPC environment.

---

## Expected structure

```
modules/
└── my_tool.nf              # process with container 'registry/image:tag'

exercises/XX_module/
└── containers/
    ├── my_tool.def         # Apptainer definition (versioned in git)
    └── README.md           # pull/build instructions
└── tests/
    ├── container_my_tool.bats      # bats tests for the container alone
    └── my_tool_container.nf.test   # nf-test with apptainer profile
```

---

## Skip pattern in bats

Container tests require a `.sif` image present locally.
Use `skip` to avoid failures in standard CI:

```bash
require_image() {
    if [ ! -f "${IMAGE}" ]; then
        skip "SIF not found — pull: apptainer pull ${IMAGE} docker://..."
    fi
}

@test "correct version" {
    require_image
    run apptainer exec "${IMAGE}" fastqc --version
    [ "$status" -eq 0 ]
    [[ "$output" =~ "FastQC v0.12.1" ]]
}
```

---

## Container CI

Container tests do not run in standard CI.
They run via `workflow_dispatch` on a self-hosted runner with Apptainer.

See `.github/workflows/test.yml`, job `test-containers`.
